subject: Statistics
title: Single-Blind vs. Double-Blind
description: A quick win: understand Single-Blind vs. Double-Blind.

hook.text: A doctor who knows which patient got the drug unconsciously treats them differently. Bias creeps in unseen.

buildup.text: In single-blind trials, patients don't know their group. In double-blind, neither patients nor doctors know.

discovery.text: Double-blinding prevents both placebo effects in patients and observation bias in researchers.

twist.text: Some treatments can't be blinded — surgery vs. no surgery, for instance. These trials require extra safeguards.

climax.text: Triple-blind trials also hide group assignments from the data analysts. Every layer removes a source of bias.

punchline.text: The less anyone knows, the more honest the results. Blinding is clarity.

quiz.question: What does double-blinding prevent in clinical trials?
options: Both patient placebo effects and researcher observation bias
Patients from dropping out of the study
The drug from having side effects
